comment_text.sn35.61:1.1: Upādāna has the primary meaning of “grasping”, and secondarily “fuel” (SN 12.52). Here the usage with pariyādāna (“depletion”) shows the metaphor of “fuel” is active; you can’t “deplete grasping”.

root_text.sn35.61:1.4: Cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn35.61:1.6: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.61:1.7: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimokkhā ‘pariyādinnaṁ me upādānan’ti pajānāti …pe…
root_text.sn35.61:1.8: jivhañca paṭicca rase ca uppajjati jivhāviññāṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.61:1.9: manañca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn35.61:1.11: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako manasmimpi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati, manoviññāṇepi nibbindati manosamphassepi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.61:1.12: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimokkhā ‘pariyādinnaṁ me upādānan’ti pajānāti.

translation_text.sn35.61:0.3: The Depletion of All Fuel for Grasping (1st)
translation_text.sn35.61:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the principle for depleting all fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.3: And what is the principle for depleting all fuel for grasping?
translation_text.sn35.61:1.4: Eye consciousness arises dependent on the eye and sights. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.5: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.6: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, sights, eye consciousness, eye contact, and feeling.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.7: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they are liberated, they understand: ‘I have completely depleted fuel for grasping.’
translation_text.sn35.61:1.9: Mind consciousness arises dependent on the mind and ideas. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.10: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.11: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, mind contact, and feeling.
translation_text.sn35.61:1.12: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they are liberated, they understand: ‘I have completely depleted fuel for grasping.’
translation_text.sn35.61:1.13: This is the principle for depleting all fuel for grasping.”

reference_text.sn35.61:1.1: cck18.40, dr18.38, ms13S4_181, msdiv61, pts-vp-pli4.34, vri26.37
